2604.09029.title: CONDESION-BENCH: Conditional Decision-Making of Large Language Models in Compositional Action Space
2604.09029.paper_summary: Large language models have been widely explored as decision-support tools in high-stakes domains due to their contextual understanding and reasoning capabilities. However, existing decision-making benchmarks rely on two simplifying assumptions: actions are selected from a finite set of pre-defined candidates, and explicit conditions restricting action feasibility are not incorporated into the decision-making process. These assumptions fail to capture the compositional structure of real-world actions and the explicit conditions that constrain their validity. To address these limitations, we introduce CONDESION-BENCH, a benchmark designed to evaluate conditional decision-making in compositional action space. In CONDESION-BENCH, actions are defined as allocations to decision variables and are restricted by explicit conditions at the variable, contextual, and allocation levels. By employing oracle-based evaluation of both decision quality and condition adherence, we provide a more rigorous assessment of LLMs as decision-support tools.
2604.09029.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型因其上下文理解和推理能力，已被廣泛探討作為高風險領域的決策支持工具。<lb/>然而，現有的決策基準依賴於兩個簡化假設：行動是從有限的預定候選者中選擇的，並且不將限制行動可行性的明確條件納入決策過程。<lb/>這些假設未能捕捉到現實世界行動的組合結構以及約束其有效性的明確條件。<lb/>為了解決這些限制，我們引入了CONDESION-BENCH，一個旨在評估組合行動空間中條件決策的基準。<lb/>在CONDESION-BENCH中，行動被定義為對決策變數的分配，並受到變數、上下文和分配層級的明確條件的限制。<lb/>通過採用基於預言者的評估來評估決策質量和條件遵循，我們提供了對大型語言模型作為決策支持工具的更嚴謹評估。
2604.09029.author: Yeonjun Hwang et.al.
2604.09029.authors: Yeonjun Hwang, Sungyong Park, Minju Kim, Dongha Lee, Jinyoung Yeo
